headline_drivers: Multi‑billion‑dollar AI data‑center colocation deal with Cerebras Systems (CBRS) – up to 40MW in Columbiana, Alabama; initial 10‑year term valued at ~$1.1B with total potential value up to $2.5B including renewals (announced in the past week, highlighted in Stocktwits article referenced in [1])

Cerebras IPO hype and perceived ‘NVIDIA rival’ status driving sympathy bid into DGXX as a key infrastructure partner; Cerebras expected to raise ~$4.8B at $150–$160/share in one of 2026’s largest IPOs (Reuters‑sourced details cited in [1])
Expansion/upsizing of DGXX’s at‑the‑market (ATM) equity program by $100M, bringing total to $175M (press release summarized in [1] and [2])
Earlier AI revenue validation: $19.6M, 24‑month ‘SubQ AI’ agreement, confirming initial AI Factory monetization (press release 2026‑04‑20, cited in [5])

Momentum/technical factors: stock up ~200–270% over 20–60 days with RSI in the 99th percentile, recent volume spike (3.54x normal on 2026‑05‑11) and heavy retail participation and bullish sentiment on Stocktwits (per [1])
summary: The market is mainly reacting to Digi Power X’s announcement of a very large AI data‑center colocation agreement with Cerebras Systems, a high‑profile NVIDIA rival that is about to IPO. The deal commits Cerebras to up to 40MW of compute capacity on DGXX’s Alabama campus with an initial 10‑year value of about $1.1B and up to $2.5B including renewals, which is a step‑change versus DGXX’s prior ~$8–9M quarterly revenue base and effectively re‑rates the company around hyperscale AI infrastructure. That fundamental catalyst is being amplified by Cerebras IPO enthusiasm, heavy retail interest and momentum screens, and by earlier proof of AI revenue via a $19.6M, 24‑month SubQ AI contract, with the ATM equity program upsizing showing DGXX is likely to fund rapid build‑out by issuing stock into this strength. In short, this is a small‑cap AI‑infrastructure name being repriced around a marquee, multi‑billion‑dollar customer win plus IPO‑driven hype, with strong technicals and retail flow accelerating the move.
